title: Zinc Chloride-Phosphorus Oxychloride: A New Reagent in Organic Chemistry
author: J. L. Bose
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A mixture of anhydrous zinc chloride and phosphorous oxychloride in certain proportions has been found to be surprisingly effective in a number of condensations in which the individual reagents are either ineffective or only slightly effective. This new reagent has been successfully used to bring about a number of condensations under comparatively mild conditions, sometimes even at the room temperature, providing good yields of the required products.
steps_raw: A mixture of anhydrous zinc chloride and phosphorous oxychloride in certain proportions has been found to be surprisingly effective in a number of condensations in which the individual reagents are either ineffective or only slightly effective. This new reagent has been successfully used to bring about a number of condensations under comparatively mild conditions, sometimes even at the room temperature, providing good yields of the required products.
